title: Ornithogalum volutare
intro: Ornithogalum volubile, also known as the 'Thread-leaf Star of Bethlehem', is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asparagaceae family, characterized by its unique, thread-like leaves and white, star-shaped flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Ornithogalum volubile include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Ornithogalum volubile is often prepared as a tincture or decoction, and is commonly used to treat conditions such as arthritis, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders.
taxonomy: Ornithogalum volutare is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae, specifically within the genus Ornithogalum.
